$comment: PRE-REGISTERED gate for v1.4.0-charoffset (the FIRST v0.5.0 char-offset-corpus run), authored 2026-06-12 BEFORE the run produces a checkpoint. It inherits EVERY floor from v4.4.0-boundary VERBATIM (the shipped contract) — the recipe is unchanged, only the corpus label FORMAT changed (token BIO -> #519 char-offset spans), so the v4.4.0 bars are the right hold-the-line contract. Two STATED changes encode the bridge-retirement test (no silent drift):
$comment_change_1: requires_bridge: FALSE. v4.4.0 shipped with the decode-side punctuation-gap span bridge (neural/span-bridge.ts) carrying dotted po_box (P.O./C.P./B.P.) — the token corpus could not label standalone dots, so the model shattered the span and the bridge re-merged it (measured po_box 60.4 -> 87.0). The char-offset corpus CAN label the dots as a contiguous span, so the model should learn po_box INTRINSICALLY. This gate runs the eval with the bridge OFF: if po_box holds, the bridge retires.
$comment_change_2: us.po_box_real raised 70.0 -> 89.1. The v4.4.0 floor (70) was the conservative 'alive and precise' first-run bar WITH the bridge. The retirement bar is the pre-registered 89.1 (2026-06-12 validation report): intrinsic bridge-OFF po_box must MEET-OR-BEAT the v4.4.0 bridge-ON measurement (87.0) with margin. Below 89.1 bridge-off = retirement DENIED (keep the bridge, flip requires_bridge back to true for the ship gate, and treat it as a MISS to confront — not a quiet re-baseline).
$comment_unpinned: OPEN — the validation report's bridge-retirement spec also names 'over-merge precision' and the '#518 punctuation lens'. Their numeric thresholds are NOT yet pinned here and must be set before this gate is authoritative (deliberately NOT fabricated). over-merge precision = the inverse risk of retiring the bridge (does intrinsic span-learning now OVER-merge adjacent tags that the bridge's Saint-Albans space-gap guard used to protect?); the #518 punctuation lens = the per-punctuation-class po_box/cedex breakdown the v4.4.0 audit used. Operator/DeepSeek to pin both (flagged in the night-shift report).
$comment_change_3: SUPERSEDED by $comment_d3_anchor_rederive (the 54.5/87.4 it cites were anchor-OFF artifacts). Original 2026-06-13 note, kept for provenance: fr.house_number lowered 91.0 -> 85.0 (STATED re-baseline, operator-approved, research note docs/articles/evals/2026-06-13-fr-house-number-threshold-research.md + #564). The 91 floor was inherited from v4.4.0, set against a near-single-town canonical-order FR eval where house_number was near-trivial (~97-98%). The diversified golden (#563, 56 localities, both orders) made fr.house_number the HARD postcode-first slice ('47110 City, 69 Street' — the model must NOT grab the leading postcode): v4.5.0 'scores only 54.5% on it'. Literature anchors put the realistic ceiling for reordered house_number at ~90-91% (Chinese flexible-order SOTA HOUSENO 89.83-91.30, Li et al. NAACL 2019) and show neural parsers COLLAPSE on reorder (deepparse 100->28%; libpostal overall 0.992->0.781). 85.0 = a literature-anchored floor for the hard slice. [2026-06-18 CORRECTION: that 54.5% 'collapse' was a MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT, not a real model failure — per-locale-f1.ts fed the anchor-trained model anchor-OFF (fixed d7b51748), which collapses the admin tags. Anchor-ON — the production feed — both v1.5.0 and v1.7.0 score fr.house_number 99.3, with NO reorder collapse. The whole literature-anchored ~85-90% framing solved a phantom. Re-derived to the anchor-on basis below.]
$comment_d3_anchor_rederive: D3 / #718 / #564 floor re-derivation (2026-06-18, STATED + operator-approved — a gate-contract change, NOT silent drift). ROOT CAUSE: per-locale-f1.ts fed the model anchor-OFF by default until commit d7b51748 (2026-06-18 16:15). An anchor-trained STAGE3 model scored anchor-off is OUT-OF-DISTRIBUTION and SELECTIVELY collapses the admin tags (country/region/locality/postcode) while morphology tags survive (docs/articles/evals/2026-06-18-v150-vs-v170-head-to-head.md §'Golden per-tag F1'). Every floor sourced from per-locale-f1.ts was set against a DEPRESSED number — too LOW, missing real regressions. SOURCE OF TRUTH = production-faithful (anchor-ON) v1.5.0 per-tag F1 (the production DEFAULT this gate's model ships under): head-to-head table + neural-weights-en-us/model-card.json eval.per_component_int8_shipconfig + capabilities.server (corroborating: locality 77.3, region 90.4, postcode 98.6). SCOPE: only per-locale-f1-derived floors re-derived (us.{postcode,micro,locality,region,street}, fr.{postcode,house_number,region}); affix/country/de/arena/po_box/cedex/intersection were ALREADY anchor-on (those scorers default the anchor+gazetteer feed) — bases honest, UNCHANGED. MARGIN: floor = anchor-on F1 − margin; 3pp large-support tags (covers int8↔fp32 ≤1.5pp + ~0.6-1.5pp run/eval spread, w/ buffer), 5pp fr.region (~197 golden rows, volatile). CONSERVATISM: never derive below the current floor (anchor-off only depressed; us.postcode 95.6<97.0 and fr.postcode 96.9<99.5 → KEEP the tighter current). us.country_homograph_f1 83.3 is NOT anchor-off and IS v1.5.0's real anchor-on score (head-to-head §country-homograph) — KEPT. RE-DERIVED: us.micro 87.4−3→84.0 (was 81.6); us.locality 77.3−3→74.0 (was 62.2); us.region 90.4−3→87.0 (was 80.1); us.street 80.2−3→77.0 (was 74.0); fr.house_number 99.3−3→96.0 (was 85.0 — THE correction); fr.region 41.8−5→36.0 (was 16.2). NOTE on the bridge-retirement floor: us.po_box_real 89.1 is UNCHANGED — score-affix.ts (its scorer) was always anchor-on, and the 89.1 retirement bar stands.
label: v0.5.0-bridge
